name: UNICEF USA
mission: Over eight decades, the United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF) has built an unprecedented global support system for the world's children. UNICEF relentlessly works day in and day out to deliver the essentials that give every child an equitable chance in life: health care and immunizations, safe water and sanitation, nutrition, education, emergency relief and more. UNICEF USA advances the global mission of UNICEF by rallying the American public to support the world's most vulnerable children. Together, we have helped save more children's lives than any other humanitarian organization.
theoryOfChange: UNICEF USA, through its Impact Fund for Children, has co-developed a 'Child-Lens Investment Framework'. This framework serves as a roadmap for investors to frame strategies and direct capital toward advancing child outcomes and minimizing child harm. It is described as a 'central guiding document' that includes core principles, a case for investment, an analysis of the global market landscape, and a description of the Child-Lens Investment ecosystem. Additionally, the Impact Fund reports impact through an 'Input/Output/Outcomes Framework' and by mapping each acceleration to UNICEF to the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

grantsData.purpose: SEE PART V UNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN CENTRAL AMERICA AND THE CARIBBEAN, UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON SIGNIFICANT HUMANITARIAN NEEDS RELATED TO MIGRATION, VIOLENCE, INTERNAL DISPLACEMENT, FOOD INSECURITY AND POVERTY.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.UNICEF'S WORK IN EAST ASIA IS FOCUSED ON EMERGENCY RESPONSE, AS OVER 1.6 BILLION PEOPLE IN THE REGION HAVE BEEN AFFECTED BY NATURAL DISASTERS SINCE 2000. THE REGION IS ALSO SIGNIFICANTLY AFFECTED BY THE NEGATIVE IMPACTS OF CLIMATE CHANGE. CYCLONES, FLOODS AND DROUGHTS ARE MORE FREQUENT AND MORE INTENSE THAN EVER BEFORE. UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON MITIGATING HARM IN THE WAKE OF NATURAL DISASTERS AND STRENGTHENING THE REGION'S HEALTH, NUTRITION, SOCIAL PROTECTION, AND CHILD PROTECTION INFRASTRUCTURE.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN THE WAKE OF THE WAR IN UKRAINE, FOR EXAMPLE, UNICEF IS ON THE GROUND MEETING URGENT AND ESCALATING NEEDS FOR SAFE WATER, HEALTH CARE, NUTRITION AND PROTECTION.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.UNICEF IS WORKING TO REACH THE TENS OF MILLIONS OF CHILDREN ACROSS THE MIDDLE EAST AND NORTH AFRICA ARE HEAVILY IMPACTED BY CONFLICT, THE CLIMATE CHANGE CRISIS AND OTHER PROTRACTED EMERGENCIES, INCLUDING, FOR EXAMPLE, IN AFGHANISTAN, WHERE 28.3 MILLION PEOPLE, MORE THAN 65 PERCENT OF THE POPULATION, ARE PROJECTED TO BE IN NEED OF HUMANITARIAN ASSISTANCE IN 2023.
SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN THE WAKE OF THE WAR IN UKRAINE, FOR EXAMPLE, UNICEF IS ON THE GROUND MEETING URGENT AND ESCALATING NEEDS FOR SAFE WATER, HEALTH CARE, NUTRITION AND PROTECTION.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE.IN SOUTH AMERICA, UNICEF IS FOCUSED ON SIGNIFICANT HUMANITARIAN NEEDS RELATED TO MIGRATION, VIOLENCE, INTERNAL DISPLACEMENT, FOOD INSECURITY AND POVERTY.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE. UNICEF'S WORK IN SOUTH ASIA FOCUSES ON CHILD PROTECTION, EMERGENCIES, INCLUDING RELIEF FROM FLOODING IN PAKISTAN, EDUCATION, AND DELIVERY OF CRITICAL SUPPLIES TO COMBAT THE COVID-19 PANDEMIC.

SEE PART VUNICEF WORKS IN OVER 190 COUNTRIES AND TERRITORIES TO SAVE CHILDREN'S LIVES, DEFEND THEIR RIGHTS AND HELP THEM REACH THEIR FULL POTENTIAL FROM EARLY CHILDHOOD THROUGH ADOLESCENCE. UNICEF IS WORKING TO DELIVER HUMANITARIAN ASSISTANCE TO THOSE IMPACTED BY FOOD CRISES, SCREENING AND TREATING CHILDREN SUFFERING FROM MALNUTRITION AND PROVIDING SAFE WATER, SANITATION AND HYGIENE SERVICES TO COMMUNITIES WHERE THESE BASIC RESOURCES ARE IN SHORT SUPPLY, AMONG OTHER INTERVENTIONS.

all_citations.claim: UNICEF USA holds a 97.0/100 rating and maintains a program expense ratio of 87.6%.
The organization reported total revenue of $720,404,955 and detailed its program expenses in its latest tax filings.
The organization provides donation options for urgent humanitarian crises, such as the Gaza crisis.
UNICEF USA offers various giving methods to support its global health and nutrition programs.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The organization earns a GMG score of 70/100 due to its massive scale and high financial efficiency, balanced by its role as a capacity-building entity rather than a direct service provider.
areas_for_improvement: Relatively lean working capital of 2.8 months compared to some peer organizations.
Medium donor alignment for specific niche audiences seeking high-directness interventions.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization shows medium alignment for donors, focusing on urgent global education and health crises [6][7]. As a Sadaqah-eligible organization, it provides a reliable pathway for general charitable giving toward international development and emergency relief [6].
dimension_explanations.impact: UNICEF USA demonstrates high impact through its capacity-building model, maintaining a program expense ratio of 87.6% and a high data confidence level [1][3]. While cost per beneficiary data is not explicitly provided, its Charity Navigator score of 97.0/100 reflects strong financial health and transparency [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: UNICEF USA supports global initiatives to end preventable maternal and child deaths while promoting the health and development of children worldwide.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 87.6%, ensuring the vast majority of funds reach field programs.
Strong fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.12 per $1 raised.
Exceptional financial health and accountability with a Charity Navigator score of 97.0/100.
baseline_narrative.summary: UNICEF USA operates as a major humanitarian organization focusing on health, nutrition, and Water, Sanitation & Hygiene (WASH) programs [1][3]. In the most recent fiscal period, the organization reported a total revenue of $720,404,955 to support its global mission [3].

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who value large-scale, institutional humanitarian relief and want their Sadaqah to support global child health and protection systems.

Prefer supporting established global institutions with massive reach
Looking for highly efficient fundraising operations
giving_considerations: Focus is on long-term capacity building rather than just short-term aid
Large scale means individual impact is part of a massive global pool
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors specifically looking to fulfill their annual Zakat obligation or those seeking small, grassroots community projects.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade B reflects <cite id="13">verified outcomes and a documented theory of change</cite>, though specific cost-per-beneficiary data is not fully transparent.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: UNICEF USA utilizes a <cite id="18">strong theory of change</cite> focused on capacity building to end preventable child deaths through health, nutrition, and WASH interventions.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that large-scale funding is effectively reducing child mortality and improving development outcomes.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: Global humanitarian crises in Gaza and elsewhere continue to outpace available resources.
strategic_priorities: Emergency response in Gaza and Ukraine

strengths.detail: The organization maintains a <cite id="9">97/100 overall score from Charity Navigator</cite>, reflecting top-tier governance and financial health.
UNICEF USA demonstrates elite efficiency, spending only <cite id="5">$0.11 to raise every $1</cite> in charitable contributions.
With <cite id="10">$1.6 billion in foreign grants</cite> distributed in 2023, the organization possesses an unparalleled ability to respond to global emergencies.

strengths_deep_dive: UNICEF USA's strength lies in its institutional stability and massive resource mobilization. The organization reported <cite id="11">$825 million in direct contributions</cite> in 2023, allowing it to maintain a <cite id="12">95% independent board</cite> that provides rigorous oversight. Their impact is further validated by <cite id="13">tracking specific outcome metrics</cite> through Candid, ensuring that large-scale funding translates into measurable improvements in child welfare.
rich_narrative.summary: UNICEF USA serves as a critical fundraising and advocacy arm for the United Nations Children's Fund, dedicated to supporting global initiatives that <cite id="1">end preventable maternal and child deaths</cite>. The organization operates at a massive scale, reporting <cite id="2">$829,050,932 in total revenue</cite> for the 2023 fiscal year. This funding supports essential programs across <cite id="3">more than 190 countries and territories</cite>, focusing on health, nutrition, water, education, and protection. UNICEF USA maintains a high level of operational efficiency, with a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 87.6%</cite> and a remarkably low fundraising cost of <cite id="5">$0.12 per $1 raised</cite>. Their work is particularly vital in crisis zones, providing life-saving aid in regions such as <cite id="6">Gaza, Ukraine, and Pakistan</cite>. By leveraging a capacity-building delivery model, the organization ensures that resources are used to <cite id="7">strengthen local health and development systems</cite> for long-term sustainability. The charity's commitment to transparency is evidenced by its <cite id="8">Platinum Seal of Transparency from Candid</cite> and a high <cite id="9">overall score of 97.0/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>.
